steps.title: Turn the wording into two points
steps.content: The problem gives the initial value $(0,80)$. A $40\%$ increase means multiply by $1.40$, so when $x=1$, $$y=80(1.40)=112.$$

steps.title: Find the exponential model
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $y=a(b)^x$. The starting value is $a=80$, and the growth multiplier is $b=1.40$, so $$y=80(1.40)^x.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The matching choice is the only option with initial value $80$ and multiplier $1.40$. The other choices use the wrong multiplier or start at $40$ instead of $80$, so A is correct.
